name: The other side of the border and Parque Natural de Montesinho — circular from Milhão
name_original: O outro lado da fronteira e Parque Natural de Montesinho — circular desde Milhão
region_intro: Road cycling around Milhão offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Bragança district in Portugal. The region is characterized by undulating plateaus, deep river valleys, and mountain ranges such as Serra de Montesinho and Serra da Coroa. Road cyclists can explore varied terrain, including paved routes and repurposed railway lines known as ecopistas. This area provides a rich natural environment for outdoor activities, with routes often traversing natural parks and offering scenic views. Best road cycling routes around Milhão The most popular road cycling route is From Bragança to Puebla de Sanabria — circular, a 51.8 miles (83.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 14 minutes to complete, crossing into Spain and offering varied scenery. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Montesinho Natural Park Loop from Bragança, a difficult 55.9 miles (90.0 km) path. This route provides extensive views within the Montesinho Natural Park, known for its biodiversity and varied altitudes. Local road cyclists also love the The other side of the border and Parque Natural de Montesinho — circular from Milhão, a 39.8 miles (64.0 km) trail leading through the natural park and across the border, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Road cycling around Milhão is defined by undulating plateaus, deep river valleys, and mountain ranges, with options for different ability levels. The network includes routes through natural parks and along scenic ecopistas. The routes in Milhão are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.98 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Milhão's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes can I find around Milhão?
A: The region around Milhão, within the Bragança district, offers a diverse range of road cycling routes. You'll find paths traversing undulating plateaus, deep river valleys, and mountain ranges like Serra de Montesinho and Serra da Coroa. Options include both challenging routes with significant elevation gain and easier paths, including repurposed railway lines known as ecopistas, which provide relatively flat and scenic experiences.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Milhão area?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes available around Milhão, catering to various fitness levels. This includes 1 easy route, 19 moderate routes, and 38 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners and experienced cyclists alike.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling near Milhão?
A: Based on regional information, August and September are popular times for cycling in the Bragança district. The weather during these months is generally favorable for exploring the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to mountain ranges.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Milhão?
A: Yes, there are several circular road cycling routes in the area. One notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1396107867/the-other-side-of-the-border-and-parque-natural-de-montesinho-circular-from-milhao" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The other side of the border and Parque Natural de Montesinho — circular from Milhão&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 64 km ride that takes you through the natural park and across the border.

Q: Can I find routes that cross into Spain from Milhão?
A: Absolutely. The proximity to the border means you can enjoy international rides. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1399628541/from-braganca-to-puebla-de-sanabria-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Bragança to Puebla de Sanabria — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 83.4 km route that crosses into Spain and offers varied scenery.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that pass through natural parks?
A: Yes, many routes in the Milhão area traverse natural parks, offering stunning views and rich biodiversity. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1396105644/montesinho-natural-park-loop-from-braganca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montesinho Natural Park Loop from Bragança&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 90 km path providing extensive views within the Montesinho Natural Park, known for its diverse wildlife and varied altitudes.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on road cycling routes around Milhão?
A: You can expect a mix of terrain. The region features undulating plateaus, deep river valleys, and mountain ranges. While many routes are on paved surfaces, some may include sections of repurposed railway lines (ecopistas) which are generally flatter. Expect significant elevation gains on more challenging routes, especially those in the Montesinho Natural Park.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Milhão?
A: The road cycling routes around Milhão are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views within natural parks, and the opportunity to explore both Portuguese and Spanish landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners near Milhão?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. For those looking for a gentler ride, consider exploring the local ecopistas, which are repurposed old railway lines offering relatively flat and scenic paths, such as the Fervença Bike Trail or Ecopista da Mãe d'Água in the broader Bragança area.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see while road cycling near Milhão?
A: The Bragança district offers several historical and cultural attractions that can be combined with your cycling trips. You could visit the imposing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4473005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bragança Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4715353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Domus Municipalis de Bragança&lt;/a&gt;, or the restored &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4715326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Mill&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7454239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bragança Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; is another significant landmark.

Q: Are there any routes that follow river valleys?
A: Yes, the region is characterized by beautiful river valleys. You can find routes that explore these areas, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1400114056/from-braganca-to-the-sabor-river-valley-circular-along-the-suba-to-carocedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Bragança to the Sabor River valley — circular along the Suba to Carocedo&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 90.6 km ride that offers stunning natural scenery along the Sabor River.

Q: What are ecopistas and are there any near Milhão?
A: Ecopistas are repurposed old railway lines that have been converted into scenic paths, ideal for cycling due to their relatively flat gradients. While Milhão itself is small, the broader Bragança area features several, including the Fervença Bike Trail and the Ecopista da Mãe d'Água. These provide a unique and often easier cycling experience.
tips: Since time immemorial, the river has always been a border element that has simultaneously helped separate several countries. The Manzanas River at this point is a good example!
A small town in the Aliste region, Riomanzanas is located a few kilometers from the Sierra de la Culebra. The town is close to the border with Portugal.
